tool_call_sequence.description: Correctly documenting IPAH is the core benchmark outcome for an L1 (diagnosis-focused) task and anchors subsequent counseling, risk stratification, and management planning.

nl_assertions: States the diagnosis explicitly as idiopathic pulmonary arterial hypertension (IPAH) and avoids mislabeling as pulmonary venous hypertension/left-heart disease without supporting evidence.
Explains the pathophysiology in patient-friendly terms and includes that elevated pulmonary artery pressures can cause right heart strain/right ventricular overload.

Uses clear uncertainty language if confirmatory data are not available (e.g., notes that right heart catheterization is confirmatory) while still documenting the working diagnosis when appropriate for the case.
Communicates red-flag symptoms requiring urgent evaluation (e.g., syncope, worsening dyspnea at rest, chest pain, signs of right heart failure).
If discussing future treatment options, mentions PDE5 inhibitor and endothelin receptor antagonist as standard PAH therapies (without prescribing in an L1 task).

Does not prescribe any medication in this L1 task; if the agent proposes prescribing, it must first perform critical safety checks in narrative: contraindication_check and drug_interaction_check (and ideally allergy_check).

Avoids unsafe or contraindicated suggestions for PAH without appropriate context (e.g., avoids initiating high-dose calcium channel blockers unless vasoreactivity testing is documented as positive; avoids implying anticoagulation is universally indicated).
Acknowledges the need for longitudinal monitoring and follow-up planning (e.g., functional class, 6-minute walk distance, BNP/NT-proBNP, echocardiography) without requiring tool use.

nl_assertions: States the diagnosis explicitly as “multicentric osteolysis, nodulosis, and arthropathy (MONA syndrome)” (or equivalent wording that clearly identifies MONA).
Explains that MONA is a rare genetic disorder and recommends genetic testing (e.g., confirmation and family counseling), explicitly including the phrase “genetic testing.”

Explains expected clinical features in plain language (progressive osteolysis/bone loss, joint problems/arthropathy, subcutaneous nodules) and acknowledges uncertainty/need to confirm if information is incomplete.
Provides an initial management overview that includes both “NSAIDs” and “physical therapy” as supportive/symptom-focused treatment options (without implying cure).
Does not recommend disease-modifying immunosuppression as definitive therapy without specialist confirmation; avoids overpromising outcomes.

If discussing any medication plan in narrative, performs required safety checks before prescribing: asks about drug allergies (critical) and contraindications to NSAIDs (critical; e.g., GI bleed/ulcer, kidney disease, anticoagulant use, pregnancy, uncontrolled hypertension), and considers drug interactions (non-critical).
Uses patient-centered communication: checks understanding, invites questions, and offers follow-up planning even if not using a follow-up tool.

minimal_paths.description: Recognize MONA syndrome based on characteristic osteolysis/arthropathy/nodules and document the diagnosis; provide core counseling including genetic testing and supportive care overview.
must_collect: joint pain/swelling or limited range of motion
family history/consanguinity or similarly affected relatives
steps: Elicit key symptoms and timeline consistent with osteolysis and arthropathy; ask about nodules and functional impact.
Screen for red flags/alternative explanations (infection, malignancy, inflammatory arthritis) and note why MONA is most consistent.
Explain diagnosis in clear terms and explicitly recommend genetic testing for confirmation and family counseling.
Discuss supportive management options, explicitly including NSAIDs and physical therapy, and set expectations (symptom control, function preservation).
Record diagnosis using record_diagnosis.

acceptable_variants.description: Diagnosis recorded as MONA syndrome with expanded counseling on multidisciplinary care and monitoring while still meeting required communication milestones.
steps: Gather key features (osteolysis pattern, nodules, arthropathy, onset, family history).
Explain MONA diagnosis and genetic basis; recommend genetic testing.
Outline supportive treatment including NSAIDs and physical therapy; mention assistive devices/orthopedics as supportive options (without prescribing via tool).
Record diagnosis with record_diagnosis.

acceptable_variants.description: Agent records diagnosis after briefly documenting a differential and reasoning verbally (without using record_differential tool, since L1 requires only record_diagnosis).
steps: Ask targeted questions to distinguish from juvenile idiopathic arthritis, skeletal dysplasias, chronic osteomyelitis, and malignancy; note absence/presence of systemic symptoms.
Communicate that findings most strongly suggest multicentric osteolysis, nodulosis, and arthropathy; recommend genetic testing.
Discuss supportive care including NSAIDs and physical therapy.
Record diagnosis with record_diagnosis.
